back_translation: And the boy Samuel was ministering the LORD before the face of Eli, and the word of the LORD was precious in those days — there was no vision spreading.
It was in that day, and Eli was lying down in his place, and his eyes had begun to grow dim — he was not able to see.
The lamp of God had not yet gone out, and Samuel was lying down in the temple of the LORD where the ark of God was.
The LORD called to Samuel, and he said, 'Behold me.'
He ran to Eli and said, 'Behold me, for you have called me.' He said, 'I did not call — return, lie down.' So he went and lay down.
The LORD again called Samuel, and Samuel rose and went to Eli and said, 'Behold me, for you have called me.' He said, 'I did not call, my son — return, lie down.'
Now Samuel did not yet know the LORD, and the word of the LORD had not yet been revealed to him.
The LORD again called Samuel a third time, and he rose and went to Eli and said, 'Behold me, for you have called me.' Then Eli understood that the LORD was calling to the boy.
Eli said to Samuel, 'Go lie down, and it shall be if he calls you, you shall say, Speak, O LORD, for your servant is listening.' So Samuel went and lay down in his place.
The LORD came and stood and called as time after time, 'Samuel, Samuel.' Samuel said, 'Speak, for your servant is listening.'
The LORD said to Samuel, 'Behold, I am doing a thing in Israel which everyone who hears of it — both his ears shall tingle.'
'In that day I will establish against Eli all that I have spoken to his house — beginning and ending.'
'I will declare to him that I am judging his house forever for the iniquity that he knows, because his sons were cursing themselves and he did not restrain them.'
'Therefore I have sworn to the house of Eli: if the iniquity of the house of Eli shall be atoned for by sacrifice or offering forever.'
Samuel lay down until morning, and he opened the doors of the house of the LORD, and Samuel was afraid to tell the vision to Eli.
Eli called Samuel and said, 'Samuel, my son.' He said, 'Behold me.'
He said, 'What is the word that he spoke to you? Do not hide from me. Thus may God do to you, and thus add, if you hide from me a word from any of the words that he spoke to you.'
Samuel told him all the words and did not hide from him, and he said, 'The LORD is he — what is good in his eyes let him do.'
Samuel grew, and the LORD was with him, and he did not let fall to the ground any of his words.
All Israel from Dan to Beersheba knew that Samuel was confirmed as a prophet to the LORD.
The LORD again appeared at Shiloh, because the LORD revealed himself to Samuel in Shiloh by the word of the LORD.
